title: PM Modi Visits Seychelles as Guest of Honour at Golden Jubilee Celebrations
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended Seychelles' Golden Jubilee (50th Independence Day) celebrations as Guest of Honour, marking a significant bilateral engagement. Modi hailed India-Seychelles ties as 'steady, strong and long-lasting,' while the Seychelles Foreign Minister described India as a 'special friend' that has 'never pressured us.' INS Ikshak was deployed to Victoria to participate in the national day celebrations. Modi also met Jonathan, widely considered the world's oldest living land animal (a giant tortoise, estimated 190+ years old).
exam_angle: Relevant for India's 'Neighbourhood First' and SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine in the Indian Ocean Region; Seychelles is a key Indian Ocean island nation with strategic importance for maritime security.
key_facts: Seychelles celebrated its 50th Independence Day (Golden Jubilee)
INS Ikshak participated in Seychelles National Day celebrations at Victoria port
Seychelles FM called India a 'special friend' that never pressures
Jonathan the tortoise (~190 years old) is the world's oldest known living land animal

title: US Secretary of State Rubio Confirms Trump Plans India Visit in Early 2027
summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that President Donald Trump is planning to visit India 'early next year' (2027), calling the Modi-Trump relationship 'great' and describing US officials as 'enormous fans of PM Modi.' Rubio also indicated that the India-US bilateral trade deal has only 'a handful of issues left' and is near completion. The US envoy further stated that H-1B visa changes are 'not targeted at India.'
exam_angle: Critical for India-US relations; connects to ongoing bilateral trade negotiations, H-1B visa policy affecting Indian professionals, and the broader strategic partnership under the Modi-Trump diplomatic framework.
key_facts: Trump expected to visit India in early 2027 as confirmed by Secretary of State Rubio
India-US trade deal described as near completion with 'handful of issues left'
H-1B visa changes stated to be 'not targeted at India'
US envoy described Modi and Trump as having similar leadership styles
source: India Today / NDTV / The New Indian Express

title: India-China Border Trade via Lipulekh Yet to Resume Despite June 1 Reopening
summary: Despite the Lipulekh pass being officially reopened for bilateral trade on June 1, actual cross-border trade between India and China via this route has yet to take off. Lipulekh pass in Uttarakhand is one of the designated border trade points between the two countries. The slow resumption highlights procedural and logistical hurdles even after the diplomatic decision to reopen the route following the resolution of border tensions.
exam_angle: Relevant to India-China relations, border trade agreements, and the strategic significance of Lipulekh — which is also disputed by Nepal; connects to GS Paper 2 topics on India's neighbourhood policy and border management.
key_facts: Lipulekh pass reopened for India-China trade on June 1
Located in Uttarakhand; one of designated India-China border trade points
Actual trade has not commenced despite official reopening
Lipulekh is also claimed by Nepal — a trilateral strategic flashpoint

title: Bangladesh-China Joint Statement: Implications of Mongla Port Deal for India
summary: A Bangladesh-China joint statement has raised strategic concerns for India, with particular focus on China's growing involvement at Mongla Port — Bangladesh's second-largest seaport on the Bay of Bengal. Analysts note that Chinese access to Mongla Port could potentially be used for dual-use (civilian and military) purposes, adding to India's encirclement concerns given China's presence at Hambantota (Sri Lanka), Gwadar (Pakistan), and Chittagong (Bangladesh). This is part of China's broader 'String of Pearls' strategy.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to India's strategic environment in the Bay of Bengal, China's 'String of Pearls' strategy, and India's concern about being encircled; connects to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Internal Security).
key_facts: Mongla Port is Bangladesh's second-largest seaport, located on the Bay of Bengal
Bangladesh-China joint statement gives China enhanced access/involvement at Mongla
Fits into China's 'String of Pearls' strategy surrounding India
Other Chinese-linked ports: Hambantota (Sri Lanka), Gwadar (Pakistan), Chittagong (Bangladesh)

title: US Sanctions Indian Firm SBL Energy and CEO for Alleged Role in Sudan Civil War
summary: The United States imposed sanctions on eight entities, including Indian firm SBL Energy and its CEO, for allegedly 'fuelling' the ongoing civil war in Sudan. The sanctions were announced by the US Treasury Department under its Sudan-related sanctions framework. This marks a rare instance of an Indian company being directly sanctioned by the US for activities in a third country's conflict, raising concerns about compliance and India-US relations.
exam_angle: Relevant for India-US relations, India's foreign policy stance on African conflicts, and US extraterritorial sanctions policy; also connects to the Sudan civil war involving the RSF (Rapid Support Forces) vs. SAF conflict.
key_facts: US sanctioned 8 entities including Indian firm SBL Energy and its CEO
Sanctions reason: alleged role in 'fuelling' Sudan's civil war
Sanctions imposed by US Treasury Department
Sudan has been in civil war between SAF (Sudanese Armed Forces) and RSF since April 2023

title: Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Framework: Netanyahu Calls Deal 'Historic'
summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu described a newly agreed ceasefire framework with Lebanon as 'historic' for Israel. However, the deal has divided Lebanese public opinion, fuelling protests in Beirut. Despite the framework agreement, Israel killed at least one person in Lebanon the day after the deal was announced, raising questions about compliance. The deal is seen as a potential step toward ending the Israel-Hezbollah conflict that escalated in 2024.
exam_angle: Relevant for West Asia geopolitics, India's significant diaspora in the region, India's abstention/vote patterns at UNASC on Israel-Palestine/Lebanon issues, and oil price implications for India.
key_facts: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire framework described as 'historic' by Netanyahu
Deal has divided Lebanese public opinion; protests erupted in Beirut
Concerns raised as Israel killed a person in Lebanon the day after agreement
Conflict involved Israel and Hezbollah, backed by Iran

title: VB-GRAMG Scheme: BJP States Raise Concerns Over Wage Burden as MGNREGA Replacement
summary: Two BJP-ruled states have questioned the wage burden implications of the proposed VB-GRAMG (Viksit Bharat–Gram Rozgar Aur Majduri Guarantee) scheme, which is being positioned as a replacement for MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act). The CITU (Centre of Indian Trade Unions) has backed a nationwide protest scheduled for July 1 against replacing MGNREGA with VB-GRAMG. The new scheme reportedly brings new norms for inclusion of Divyangjan (persons with disabilities). Punjab has reportedly done a U-turn on the scheme.
exam_angle: MGNREGA is one of the most important rural employment programmes in India (GS Paper 2 & 3); any move to replace or restructure it is highly exam-relevant; connects to welfare schemes, federalism (Centre-State), and labour rights.
key_facts: VB-GRAMG (Viksit Bharat–Gram Rozgar Aur Majduri Guarantee) proposed as replacement for MGNREGA
CITU called for July 1 nationwide protest against the replacement
New scheme includes specific norms for Divyangjan inclusion
Two BJP-led states have publicly questioned the wage burden under the new scheme

title: India Launches World's First Nuclear-Powered Hydrogen Production Facility
summary: India has started the world's first hydrogen production facility using nuclear technology, marking a landmark achievement in clean energy. The facility uses nuclear power as the energy source for hydrogen production, potentially offering a cost-effective and low-carbon route to green/pink hydrogen. This positions India as a global pioneer in nuclear hydrogen technology and aligns with the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech and Energy); connects to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, nuclear energy policy (DAE), and India's climate commitments under NDCs/COP.
key_facts: India started the world's FIRST hydrogen production facility using nuclear technology
Hydrogen produced using nuclear power = 'pink hydrogen'
Aligns with India's National Green Hydrogen Mission
Relevant ministry: Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) / Ministry of New & Renewable Energy

title: Pax Silica Summit: India Among 35 Nations Backing US-Led 'AI Opportunity' Declaration
summary: India was among 35 countries that backed the US-led 'AI Opportunity' declaration at the Pax Silica Summit. The declaration focuses on promoting artificial intelligence for economic opportunity and development, with participating nations committing to frameworks for responsible AI deployment. The summit represents a US-led effort to shape global AI governance norms in competition with alternative frameworks.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's AI policy (IndiaAI Mission), global AI governance debates, and India's alignment with Western democratic nations on technology norms; GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS Paper 2 (IR).
key_facts: Summit name: Pax Silica Summit
35 countries backed the US-led 'AI Opportunity' declaration including India
Declaration focuses on AI for economic opportunity and development
India also has its own IndiaAI Mission (launched 2024, Rs 10,371.92 crore budget)

title: OpenAI Announces New AI Models; US Government Restricts Broad Release
summary: OpenAI announced new advanced AI models named Sol, Terra, and Luna (also referred to as GPT-5.6 models), but the US government blocked their wide/public release citing national security concerns, requiring the company to brief the government before deployment. Separately, the US also allowed limited, controlled access to Anthropic's Mythos AI model after an earlier security review-related block. These developments highlight growing government oversight of frontier AI systems.
exam_angle: Relevant to AI governance, national security dimensions of AI, and India's own regulatory approach through the IndiaAI Mission; connects to debates on AI safety and export controls in GS Paper 3.
key_facts: OpenAI announced Sol, Terra, Luna AI models (GPT-5.6 series)
US government blocked wide release of OpenAI's new models over national security concerns
Anthropic's Mythos AI model given limited access after security review
Trend: US government increasingly involved in oversight of frontier AI model releases

title: Indian Navy's Eastern Fleet Ships Arrive at Sattahip, Thailand for Bilateral Engagement
summary: Three Indian Navy ships from the Eastern Fleet — INS Udaygiri, INS Kavaratti, and INS Shakti — arrived at Sattahip Naval Base in Thailand as part of bilateral naval engagement. This deployment reflects India's Act East Policy and efforts to deepen maritime security cooperation with ASEAN nations. Sattahip is the headquarters of the Royal Thai Navy.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's Act East Policy, maritime diplomacy, and naval outreach in the Indo-Pacific; INS Udaygiri is a guided missile frigate and INS Kavaratti is an anti-submarine warfare corvette — both indigenously built, relevant to Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence.
key_facts: Ships deployed: INS Udaygiri (frigate), INS Kavaratti (ASW corvette), INS Shakti (fleet tanker)
Destination: Sattahip Naval Base, Thailand (Royal Thai Navy HQ)
Part of Indian Navy Eastern Fleet deployment
Aligns with India's Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific maritime strategy

title: Myanmar Overtakes Afghanistan as Top Illicit Opium Source; NCB Flags India Risk
summary: Myanmar has overtaken Afghanistan as the world's largest source of illicit opium, according to new data flagged by India's Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB). The shift follows a drastic reduction in Afghan opium production after the Taliban government's 2022 ban on poppy cultivation. Myanmar's Golden Triangle region, which borders India's northeastern states, poses a direct narcotics trafficking risk for India. NCB has specifically flagged the heightened risk to India given the porous borders with Myanmar.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's internal security (drug trafficking from Myanmar's Golden Triangle through India's northeast), NCB's role, and India-Myanmar border management; also connects to UNODC reports on global drug trends.
key_facts: Myanmar has overtaken Afghanistan as the world's top illicit opium producer
Afghanistan production dropped sharply after Taliban's 2022 poppy ban
Myanmar's Golden Triangle borders India's northeastern states (Manipur, Nagaland, Mizoram)
India's NCB has flagged heightened drug trafficking risk from Myanmar

title: Aravalli Conservation: Supreme Court Examines India's Oldest Mountain Range Protection
summary: The Supreme Court of India is examining the issue of conservation of the Aravalli mountain range, the world's oldest fold mountain range. The case involves illegal mining, encroachment, and deforestation in the Aravalli hills spanning Rajasthan, Haryana, Gujarat, and Delhi. The Court's intervention is being seen as a critical moment for protecting the ecological and hydrological functions of the Aravalli range, which acts as a natural barrier against the Thar Desert's spread.
exam_angle: Relevant to environment and ecology (GS Paper 3), Supreme Court's role in environmental protection, and the Aravalli's significance as India's oldest mountain range and ecological buffer against desertification.
key_facts: Aravalli range is the world's oldest fold mountain range
Spans Rajasthan, Haryana, Gujarat, and Delhi
Acts as ecological barrier preventing spread of Thar Desert
Supreme Court is examining conservation and protection measures

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank MD & CEO Ashok Vaswani to Step Down by Year-End
summary: Ashok Vaswani, the Managing Director and CEO of Kotak Mahindra Bank, has announced his resignation for personal reasons, effective by the end of 2026. The bank is expected to submit a shortlist of successor candidates to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) for approval, as RBI approval is mandatory for CEO appointments in private sector banks. Two internal candidates are reportedly being considered as replacements.
exam_angle: Relevant to RBI's regulatory powers over private sector banks (Banking Regulation Act, 1949), the process of CEO appointments in banks requiring RBI prior approval, and corporate governance in banking sector.
key_facts: Ashok Vaswani to resign as MD & CEO of Kotak Mahindra Bank by end of 2026
RBI approval is mandatory for CEO/MD appointments in private sector banks
Legal basis: Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (Section 35B)
Kotak Mahindra Bank is India's 4th largest private sector bank by assets

title: Maharashtra TET Postponed Day Before Exam After Paper Leak, 3 Arrested
summary: The Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) was postponed just one day before it was scheduled to be held, following suspicion of a paper leak. Three persons were arrested in connection with the alleged leak. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi described the incident as 'theft of youth's future.' The incident has triggered political controversy, with Uddhav Thackeray's faction attacking the BJP-led Maharashtra government over the matter.
exam_angle: Relevant to issues of examination integrity, governance failures in public recruitment/examination systems, and the need for a dedicated anti-paper leak law (Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024 provides context).
key_facts: Maharashtra TET postponed one day before exam due to suspected paper leak
3 persons arrested in connection with the alleged leak
Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024 was passed by Parliament to address exam fraud
TET qualification is mandatory for appointment as a school teacher under RTE Act

title: Ram Temple Trust General Secretary and Member Resign Amid Donation Controversy
summary: The General Secretary and a member of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust resigned following a controversy over alleged theft of donations at the Ayodhya Ram Temple. Eight persons were arrested for allegedly stealing donations. The controversy triggered sharp political reactions, with opposition leaders including Uddhav Thackeray accusing the BJP of 'betrayal of Hindus,' while Akhilesh Yadav vowed to 'restore Ayodhya's glory.'
exam_angle: Relevant to institutional governance of religious trusts, accountability of quasi-public bodies, and politically significant given the Ram Temple's construction under Supreme Court's 2019 Ayodhya verdict; touches on charitable trust regulation.
key_facts: Trust involved: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust
General Secretary and one member resigned amid donation theft controversy
8 persons arrested for allegedly stealing donations at the temple
Trust was constituted by the Central Government following the Supreme Court's November 2019 Ayodhya verdict
source: The Hindu / The Times of India

title: CBI Attaches Rs 53 Crore Assets of Former Assam Top Cop in Corruption Case
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) attached assets worth Rs 53 crore belonging to a former top police officer of Assam in connection with a corruption case. The attachment was carried out under the Prevention of Corruption Act. This action underscores the CBI's role in investigating high-level corruption among public servants.
exam_angle: Relevant to anti-corruption mechanisms, CBI's powers of asset attachment, Prevention of Corruption Act provisions, and accountability of public servants; important for GS Paper 2 (Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Economy).
key_facts: CBI attached assets worth Rs 53 crore of a former top Assam police officer
Case involves corruption charges
Legal basis: Prevention of Corruption Act / PMLA provisions for attachment
CBI is India's premier investigation agency under the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946

title: Manipur: Kuki Body Apologises for Killing 6 Naga Hostages; Civil Groups Seek Action
summary: A Kuki militant body issued an apology for killing six Naga hostages in Manipur, triggering demands from civil society groups for concrete action and accountability. The incident reflects the continuing inter-ethnic tensions in Manipur, where violence between Kuki and Meitei communities has persisted since May 2023. The killing of Naga hostages adds a new ethnic dimension to the already complex conflict.
exam_angle: Relevant to internal security, ethnic conflicts in Northeast India, role of militant groups, and Centre's responsibility under Article 355/356; connects to broader Manipur crisis that has been ongoing since May 2023.
key_facts: Kuki militant body apologised for killing 6 Naga hostages in Manipur
Manipur has been experiencing ethnic violence between Kuki and Meitei communities since May 2023
Incident adds Naga dimension to the existing Kuki-Meitei conflict
Article 355 mandates Union to protect states against internal disturbance

title: Air India May Restore Some International Flights as Gulf Tensions Ease
summary: Air India is considering restoring some international flight routes, particularly to Gulf countries, as tensions in the West Asian region show signs of easing following the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire framework. Several Indian airlines had suspended or rerouted flights due to the conflict, impacting connectivity for the large Indian diaspora in Gulf nations. The resumption of flights would benefit trade and travel between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries.
exam_angle: Relevant to India-Gulf relations, Indian diaspora (largest in the world concentrated in GCC), remittances to India (Gulf accounts for a major share), and impact of West Asia conflict on India's economy.
key_facts: Air India considering resuming Gulf international routes suspended due to West Asia conflict
India has the world's largest diaspora, heavily concentrated in GCC countries
Gulf countries account for a significant share of India's inward remittances (~$30 billion annually from GCC)
Trigger for resumption: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire framework

title: MDMK Quits DMK-Led Alliance in Tamil Nadu After 9 Years
summary: The Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (MDMK), led by Vaiko, has quit the DMK-led alliance in Tamil Nadu after nine years, marking a setback for Chief Minister MK Stalin's party. MDMK is a regional Dravidian party with a significant voter base in Tamil Nadu. The split could affect the alliance arithmetic in the state ahead of upcoming elections.
exam_angle: Relevant to understanding regional party dynamics in Tamil Nadu, coalition politics in Indian democracy, and the Dravidian party system — important for GS Paper 2 (Indian Polity) and current affairs on state politics.
key_facts: MDMK (Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam) quit DMK-led alliance after 9 years
MDMK is led by Vaiko (V. Gopalasamy)
Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin leads the DMK
Development seen as a setback for the ruling DMK's alliance consolidation
